key_drivers: Real yields falling sharply (10Y TIPS at 1.80%, down ~15-20bps from prior week) — dominant bullish driver for gold and growth assets
Gold ETF inflows: 9th consecutive month of inflows, $5.3B in February 2026 per World Gold Council — strong institutional demand
Iran war geopolitical risk elevated: VIX at 23.75, Strait of Hormuz supply disruption, oil prices mid-$70s — bullish CL and GC, risk-off for AUD
BOJ hiking cycle (0.75%) + Fed neutral hold (3.50-3.75%) = JPY rate differential narrowing, safe-haven demand — bullish 6J
Credit spreads widening (HY OAS at 2.97%, up from 2.5% lows) + SOX falling (-4.65% 1-month) + MOVE rising (+25.68% 1-month) — headwinds for equities
China official PMI contracted to 49.0 (2nd consecutive month) + risk-off environment — double-weighted bearish for AUD (6A)
ECB dovish hold vs Fed neutral hold: rate differential narrowing vs USD — M6E neutral/slight bearish
NFP report due today (March 6, 8:30am ET): consensus 59-60K jobs; miss could spark recession fears, beat could delay Fed cuts
